item.title: Технические статьи
item.description: Исследования экспертов BI.ZONE и практические материалы о кибербезопасности

item.title: Utilizing adversary infrastructure insights to derail attacks
item.description: This article explains how blocking threat actors' infrastructure can disrupt their control over malware and prevent further impact

item.title: Technical articles
item.description: BI.ZONE expert research and practical materials on cybersecurity

item.title: Approaching cyclone: Vortex Werewolf attacks Russia
item.description: A new threat actor leverages phishing to spread malware. In this article, we examine a Vortex Werewolf attack that employs webpages mimicking file downloads in Telegram

item.title: Adversaries exploit CVE-2025-55182 to attack Russian companies
item.description: In most cases, the attacks aim to deploy the XMRig miner. However, threat actors also deliver implants, botnets, and other malware

item.title: Arcane Werewolf revamps its arsenal with Loki 2.1 implant
item.description: The cluster targets Russian manufacturing companies

item.title: Leveraging darknet to predict attacks
item.description: In the third article of our cycle of publications on actionable threat intelligence, we delve into the insights that can be derived from darknet ads

item.title: Mapping and leveraging cyber threat landscape to predict attacks
item.description: This is the second article in our series on using cyber intelligence insights. This time, BI.ZONE Threat Intelligence explores how knowing and understanding adversaries, their methods and tools help build stronger IT infrastructure defenses

item.title: Embracing adversary-centric approach to avoid impact
item.description: This is an introductory article in our series on leveraging cyber intelligence insights in everyday practice. BI.ZONE Threat Intelligence explores a cybersecurity approach that allows organizations to prioritize the most relevant threats

item.title: Digging into CVE-2025-62592 in Oracle VirtualBox for macOS
item.description: We uncover a virtualization software vulnerability causing an ASLR leak

item.title: Cavalry Werewolf raids Russia's public sector with trusted relationship attacks
item.description: The malicious actors pose as government officials and utilize own crafted malware in their attacks

item.title: Technical articles
item.description: BI.ZONE expert research and practical materials on cybersecurity
